agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: RCMP Lower Mainland District integrated team changes in Burnaby
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will hear a presentation from RCMP senior officers about proposed changes to the Lower Mainland District (LMD) Integrated Teams and a position reconciliation plan (Annex A). This is an invited presentation at a council meeting — no decision has been made yet. The presentation will explain how the RCMP's integrated teams (specialized units that work across multiple police jurisdictions) are currently staffed and how positions might be reorganized. Business owners should be aware that changes to police team structures could affect response times or community safety resources in Burnaby, but nothing has been approved or enacted at this stage.
agenda_briefs.action_required: No immediate action needed — monitor council meeting minutes for any future decisions that could affect local policing resources.
agenda_briefs.industries: Professional Services, Retail, Landlord & Property Mgmt
agenda_briefs.source_body:
5.1 INVITED PRESENTATION: RCMP - LMD Integrated Teams and Annex A Position Reconciliation
Presenters:
C/Supt. Bert Ferreira, OIC Lower Mainland District Integrated Teams, and Insp. Chad Greig, ITeams Planning & Performance Officer
agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — June 25, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to receive overview of Burnaby RCMP Youth Services Unit
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will hear a verbal report from staff about the RCMP Youth Services Unit. The presentation will cover the unit's work, including youth and family therapy and restorative justice programs. This is an information item only — no bylaw or policy change is being voted on at this time.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.1 VERBAL REPORT: RCMP YOUTH SERVICES UNIT
Purpose: To provide Committee with an overview of the Burnaby RCMP Youth Services Unit.
Staff presentation by Bahareh Hossein-Pour, Unit Supervisor and Youth and Family Therapist, and Justin Hohne, Youth Restorative Justice Coordinator
agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — June 25, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider RCMP policing update and performance report
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will review the RCMP status update for February–March 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan quarterly report for January–March 2026. The report shows shoplifting increased 34% (983 to 1,314 cases) and break & enter to business increased 27% (92 to 117 cases) compared to the same period in 2025. Traffic officers conducted 132 enforcement operations including 57 speeding and 18 distracted driving operations. The online crime reporting system continues to receive about 200 reports per period.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.2 SU: RCMP STATUS REPORT (FEBRUARY - MARCH 2026) AND ANNUAL PERFORMANCE PLAN QUARTERLY REPORT (JANUARY - MARCH 2026)

SU - RCMP Status Report (February –March 2026) 2026-06-25.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 – RCMP PSC Report (February - March 2026) 2026-06-25.pdf
3.
Attachment 2 – APP Quarterly Report (January – March 2026) 2026-06-25.pdf
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on policing operations covering February to March 2026 and the Annual Performance Plan (APP) quarterly report covering January to March 2026.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Fire Department 2025 Annual Report for Council review
agenda_briefs.summary: Council will consider the Burnaby Fire Department's 2025 Annual Report at an upcoming meeting. This report covers the department's activities, response statistics, and key achievements up to the end of December 2025. While the report itself does not propose new rules or fees, it may inform future decisions on fire safety, emergency services, and related bylaws that could affect local businesses. Business owners should review the report to understand any trends or recommendations that might impact their operations or insurance requirements.
agenda_briefs.action_required: Review the Burnaby Fire Department 2025 Annual Report (attached to the agenda) to stay informed about fire safety trends and potential future impacts on your business.
agenda_briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Food Service

Purpose: To provide the Burnaby Fire Department's Annual Report available to the end of December 2025.

agenda_briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Fire Department status update for April 2026
agenda_briefs.summary: The Public Safety Committee received the Burnaby Fire Department's status update covering operations through April 2026. The report shows total incidents rose 2.5% to 17,899 in 2025, with medical emergencies accounting for 56.7% of calls. Major incidents included a 2nd-alarm structure fire at 6190 Walker Avenue on March 20, 2026, and a 3rd-alarm fire at 6622 Willingdon Avenue on April 18, 2026. The dry wildland season now runs April through October.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.4 SU: FIRE DEPARTMENT (APRIL 2026)

SU - Fire Department (April 2026) 2026-06-25.pdf
2.
Attachment 1 – SU - Fire Department Status Report 2026-06-25.pdf
Purpose: To provide the Burnaby Fire Department's status update (SU) available to the end of April 2026.

agenda_briefs.article_title: Public Safety Committee Meeting Agenda — June 25, 2026

agenda_briefs.title: Council to consider update on Community Safety Advisory Committee work
agenda_briefs.summary: Burnaby Council will review a status update on the work of the Community Safety Advisory Committee (CSAC) for May and June 2026. The report identifies specific safety concerns: a significant increase in shoplifting at Winners, Save On Foods and Whole Foods in the Brentwood/SOLO district and along Hastings Street; an e-scooter accident involving two children at Hythe and Hastings on May 2, 2026; a noticeable increase in graffiti; and open drug use in Broadview Park and along the bike trail near the Coast hotel at Boundary and Grandview. The committee also noted vehicle incidents near Burnaby Urgent Care on Kingsway and crosswalk safety issues on Mary Ave near Vista Care and on Rumble St near Ron McLean Park. This is a proposed update for information only — no new rules or decisions have been made yet.
agenda_briefs.source_body:
7.5 SU: COMMUNITY SAFETY ADVISORY COMMITTEE (MAY - JUNE 2026)

SU - CSAC D1 - 2026-06-25.pdf

Purpose:
To provide a status update (SU) on the work of Community Safety Advisory Committees (CSAC) for May - June 2026.
